description: Need vm.swappiness dropped to 10 on this box — currently at the default 60. Make it persistent across reboots too, not just a runtime tweak. Config should live in /etc/sysctl.d/99-swap.conf.
truth:
Initial state:
- Ubuntu 22.04 system, agent user has sudo privileges via NOPASSWD for sysctl commands and write access to /etc/sysctl.d/
- /proc/sys/vm/swappiness currently reads 60
- /etc/sysctl.d/99-swap.conf does not exist
- /etc/sysctl.d/ directory exists and is writable by agent
- sysctl command available

Expected final state:
- /etc/sysctl.d/99-swap.conf exists and contains the line `vm.swappiness = 10` (or `vm.swappiness=10`, whitespace around = is flexible)
- `cat /proc/sys/vm/swappiness` returns 10
- The setting has been applied to the running kernel (not just written to file)

Invariants:
- No other files in /etc/sysctl.d/ modified
- /etc/sysctl.conf unchanged

Anti-shortcut guards:
- File must contain vm.swappiness, not be empty or contain unrelated settings: `grep -q 'vm.swappiness.*=.*10' /etc/sysctl.d/99-swap.conf`
- Runtime value must actually be 10: `[ "$(cat /proc/sys/vm/swappiness)" = "10" ]`
name: task_000000_ea23560a
